events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S½ NE¼, SE¼ NW¼, Lot 2, Sec. 19, T.9S., R.34E.) under Act of April 11, 1882; trust patent issued December 2, 1907 under Act of February 8, 1887
Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent signed by President Calvin Coolidge on December 21, 1923
Petition for Sale of Land by Frederick Geisdorff Jr. (original allottee) received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made an offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment land
Appraisal made pertaining to Powers' request to lease land and adjacent land owned by Geisdorff relatives; appraised value $1,194
Crow Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment for sale on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale of Uintah and Ouray Agency
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed acceptance form and returned to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 signed written acceptance of his bid of $1,500 for 159.67 acres; remittance of balance demanded
Superintendent acknowledged receipt of checks from Thomas R. Powers for balance of sale price ($1,350 and $2.00 advertising fee); letter written to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. about age issue
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza, aged about 20, married, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency notifying that Fritz (Frederick Geisdorff Jr.) died a few days ago of influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers about age issue and willingness to cancel sale, offering return of payment
Emily Lucile Geisdorff born, daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Superintendent Asbury of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote Crow Agency Superintendent inquiring if agency could aid her in paying note owed by her deceased husband
Superintendent answered Emily Geisdorff's letter, regretting no funds available, noting sale pending but uncertain due to allottee's age, promising hearing to determine heirs
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters concerning land sale, holding two checks from Powers ($1,350 and $150)
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner, asking if she wished sale completed

Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter stating nothing shows brother/sister want to sell or widow wants sale to go through; if probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner, stating he had just received acceptance of widow of allottee
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily F. Geisdorff asking whether she wished sale completed to Powers at $1,500 or bid rejected; enclosed authority to put old sale through
General Land Office stated no reservations or withdrawals covering land and no reasons why patent in fee should not be issued to purchaser; signed by John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs requesting completion of Land Sale No. 991, noting Powers requested return of deposit if nothing definite done
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers enclosing copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
Fee Patent No. 719146 issued by President Woodrow Wilson to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres from Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent No. 719146 to Crow Agency Superintendent for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signed receipt
Receipt for Patent issued at Crow Agency; Superintendent C.H. Asbury acknowledged receipt of fee patent No. 719146 for 159.67 acres
Crow Agency notified U.S. Land Office in Billings, Montana of fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Superintendent Kneale of Fort Duchesne, Utah
Report on heirship transmitted to Secretary of Interior regarding estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
Heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. determined; Emily J. Geisdorff entitled to one-half plus dower interest; Emily Lucile Geisdorff entitled to balance
Check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee; total on books $1,521.24
Report dated June 11, 1979 alleging land was illegally sold to non-Indian when upon death of allottee land should have become property of Crow Tribe per trust patent terms and Treaty of 1868

financial_transactions.payee: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate / Crow Agency
financial_transactions.description: Purchase price for 159.67 acres of Crow allotment No. 2336 under Crow Land Sale No. 991; paid via two checks: $1,350.00 and $150.00 deposit previously submitted with bid
Check on Sheridan Banking Company for balance of purchase price of Geisdorff allotment No. 2336
Advertising fee check on Sheridan Banking Company for Crow Land Sale No. 991 (purchase price over $1,000 and under $2,000)
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Geisdorff allotment
Check No. 10613 representing Emily J. Geisdorff's share (one-half plus dower interest) of proceeds from sale of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment; total on books was $1,521.24
Share of sale proceeds credited to minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Heirship fee deducted from total estate proceeds of $1,521.24 as authorized by Indian Office
Bank account balance derived from lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment (159.67 acres) as appraised by Government Farmer

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for Geisdorff allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Answer regarding leasing of allotment land
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.subject: Acceptance of bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Remit balance due on purchase price within 30 days plus advertising fee; sign affidavit of vendee in duplicate
correspondence.outcome: Powers paid balance via checks dated November 2, 1918

correspondence.subject: Age issue regarding sale of allotment; request for birth date and relatives' names
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names/addresses of all relatives; submit statement from Superintendent Kneale recommending approval
correspondence.outcome: No reply received; allottee died November 27, 1918

correspondence.subject: Age issue with Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and potential cancellation of sale
correspondence.action_requested: Informed Powers of age issue; offered to cancel sale and return payment upon request
correspondence.outcome: Sale not immediately cancelled; Powers later requested return of deposit

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None; informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death

correspondence.subject: Request for assistance paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance to pay husband's debt
correspondence.outcome: Denied; no funds available

correspondence.subject: Response to request for financial assistance; status of pending land sale
correspondence.action_requested: Informed widow of pending sale uncertainty and upcoming heirship hearing
correspondence.outcome: Widow notified; hearing to be advertised

correspondence.subject: Review of all matters concerning Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale; request for instructions
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale given allottee's death and age issue; holding checks from Powers ($1,350 and $150)
correspondence.outcome: Washington responded April 26, 1919

correspondence.subject: Inquiry whether widow wishes pending sale completed
correspondence.action_requested: Widow to indicate whether she wants sale completed or not
correspondence.outcome: Widow eventually provided acceptance

correspondence.sender_title: Chief Clerk, Office of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Response to March 24, 1919 letter regarding Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale
correspondence.action_requested: If probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers; otherwise bid may be rejected and land readvertised after heirs determined
correspondence.outcome: Asbury proceeded with sale papers after receiving widow's acceptance

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of allotment of deceased husband Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority if she wishes sale to Powers at $1,500 to proceed; write if she does not wish sale to go through
correspondence.outcome: Widow signed acceptance

correspondence.subject: Enclosure of sale papers for allotment 2336; receipt of widow's acceptance
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991; Powers requesting return of deposit if no definite action
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at earliest date; Powers threatening to withdraw
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
correspondence.action_requested: Informational
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office
correspondence.subject: Clearance for fee patent issuance for Geisdorff allotment
correspondence.action_requested: None; clearance statement
correspondence.outcome: No reservations or withdrawals found; patent cleared for issuance

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent No. 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to Powers upon signed receipt in duplicate; forward original receipt to Washington
correspondence.outcome: Patent delivered; receipt signed December 19, 1919

correspondence.subject: Notification of fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Informational notification
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.subject: Birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff
correspondence.action_requested: Informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of heir's birth

correspondence.subject: Proceeds of sale and minor's share
correspondence.action_requested: Informed widow that proceeds can be transferred to her agency upon request after heirs determined; minor's shares cannot be paid without special authority
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.subject: Heirship to estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration of heirship report and recommendations
correspondence.outcome: Heirs determined; Emily J. Geisdorff and Emily Lucile Geisdorff as equal heirs under Utah statutes

correspondence.subject: Distribution of estate proceeds; check for widow's share
correspondence.action_requested: Receipt of check No. 10613 for $1,317.26
correspondence.outcome: Payment made to widow; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of February 8, 1887 (24 Stat. L. 388) - Dawes Act - under which trust patent for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment was issued December 2, 1907
Act of April 11, 1882 (22 Stat. L. 42) as amended - under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr. was allotted his land
Act of May 18, 1916 (39 Stat. 123) - referenced in approval of fee patent to Thomas R. Powers regarding water rights and reclamation provisions
Act of June 4, 1920 (41 Stat. 751) - under which Francis Geisdorff was allotted 648.04 acres as homestead on Crow Reservation; trust patent signed December 21, 1923
Treaty of 1868 establishing Crow Tribe property rights; cited in 1979 report as basis for argument that land should have reverted to Crow Tribe upon allottee's death
